frame_analyses.description: Static view showing laparoscopic trainer setup with instruments and materials laid out
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard FLS station setup visible with needle drivers, scissors, knot pusher, and suture material

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver bringing suture through laparoscopic port into trainer box
Needle positioned at 90-degree angle to tissue surface, preparing for penetration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Excellent perpendicular approach to tissue, optimal needle angle

frame_analyses.description: Non-dominant driver assisting with needle regrasping after initial penetration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bimanual coordination, needle being repositioned for optimal grip

frame_analyses.description: Needle being transferred from dominant to non-dominant driver
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean instrument exchange, maintaining needle control throughout transfer

frame_analyses.description: Dominant driver grasping suture approximately 2cm from needle as instructed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper suture grasp distance from needle, good positioning

frame_analyses.description: Needle being regrasped with non-dominant driver for better control
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Multiple regrasping attempts suggest some difficulty with needle control

frame_analyses.description: Needle being advanced through tissue with pronation technique to follow needle curve
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper wrist pronation to follow needle curvature, minimizing tissue trauma

frame_analyses.description: Needle positioned for final advancement through tissue, both marks targeted
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good visualization of both target marks, needle trajectory appears accurate

frame_analyses.description: Final needle positioning with non-dominant driver assistance for tissue exit
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated bimanual technique for completing suture passage

confidence_rationale: Limited frame visibility for complete task assessment. Cannot observe knot tying, knot pushing, or suture cutting phases which are critical components. Suture placement appears accurate but exact deviation measurements difficult to assess from available angles.
cannot_determine: Exact extracorporeal knot formation technique - not visible in frames
Knot pusher deployment and effectiveness - no frames showing this phase
Final suture cutting - completion not visible
Precise mm measurements for suture deviation - limited camera angles
Gap assessment in drain - insufficient close-up views
technique_summary: Excellent needle handling and suture placement technique demonstrated in visible phases. Expert-level timing for suture placement phase with good bimanual coordination and proper needle advancement using pronation technique.
strengths: Proper suture grasping technique at recommended distance
Excellent bimanual coordination throughout visible phases
improvement_suggestions: Assessment limited by incomplete video - full task completion not visible
Would benefit from observing complete knot tying sequence for comprehensive evaluation
